new_classes.definition_en: Grape must that is still in fermentation or whose fermentation has been arrested by means other than the addition of alcohol (e.g. by chilling, filtration, or SO₂ treatment), classified under CN subheading 2204 30 10 (Chapter 22). Distinguishable from CN 2204 30 92 / 2204 30 96 (grape must with fermentation arrested by addition of alcohol) by the absence of exogenous alcohol as the arresting agent.

coverage_explanation: The two primary distinguishing criteria from the legal text are captured: (1) the grape must is in fermentation or has had fermentation arrested, and (2) the arresting agent is NOT alcohol addition — modelled via the new boolean property `fermentationArrestedByAlcohol` set to false. The CN code string 22043010 is bound via `codeString`. A minor gap remains: the legal text references Chapter 22 additional notes 2(a), 2(b), 2(c) and 3, which set density/specific gravity thresholds used to delimit grape must subheadings (22043010 vs. 22043092/22043096). These density-based criteria are not yet modelled as datatype property restrictions because no `densityOrSpecificGravity` property exists in the TBox, slightly reducing coverage.
